summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Right knee pain following a fall.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports right knee pain following an accidental fall while working on his property. He describes swelling and discomfort, with a pain score of 4/10. He has been taking ibuprofen for the swelling and pain, and reports a history of hypertension managed with lisinopril 20mg daily. He acknowledges a need to improve his diet and reduce salt intake to potentially reduce his medication dependence.

Findings
Physical examination reveals pain to palpation of the medial aspect of the right knee, limited range of motion with flexion and extension, and mild edema and effusion. The patient's blood pressure was 150/70 mmHg. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally, and the heart exam reveals a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur. An X-ray showed no fractures or bony abnormalities.

Assessment
The patient presents with a medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee. He also has hypertension.

Plan
Prescribe ibuprofen 800 mg twice daily for pain and swelling. Order a knee brace for support. Recommend ice therapy three to four times daily for 20 minutes. Refer the patient to a registered dietitian for dietary counseling. Continue lisinopril 20mg daily and schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess progress. Consider a referral to nutrition services to address dietary modifications and potential medication reduction.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Right knee pain.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Cardiovascular: Denies chest pain.
Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath.
Gastrointestinal: Denies abdominal pain.
Musculoskeletal: Reports right knee pain.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Respiratory
- Auscultation of Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No wheezes.

Cardiovascular
- Auscultation of Heart: Grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur. Some edema and effusion noted around the right knee.

Musculoskeletal
- Examination: Right knee
- Palpation: Pain to palpation of the medial aspect. No pain to palpation of the lateral aspect.
- ROM: Pain with flexion and extension.
-Some effusion noted around the right knee.

RESULTS

X-rays of the right knee were taken. These show no fractures or bony abnormalities.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Right knee MCL strain.
- Medical Reasoning: Based on the physical examination findings, the patient has a MCL strain of the right knee. - Medical Treatment: I have prescribed ibuprofen 800 mg twice a day. I will also place him in a knee brace. I advised him to ice the knee for 20 minutes at a time for 3 to 4 times a day. The patient was instructed to elevate his leg as needed and avoid strenuous activities for 2-3 weeks.

2. Hypertension.
- Medical Treatment: The patient will continue lisinopril 20 mg daily. I have also provided a referral to see a nutritionist for dietary changes.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.
